output:
# SENTRY — Spatially-Adaptive, Semantic-Readiness-Triggered, Closed-Loop-Verified Relay for Tuning-Free Higher-Resolution Diffusion

**Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field — Machine Learning (tuning-free higher-resolution image synthesis with diffusion models).**

Retraining diffusion models natively at 2K/4K is prohibitive in data, compute, and carbon, so an entire active subfield — MegaFusion, ScaleCrafter, DemoFusion, FouriScale, HiDiffusion, AccDiffusion — exists to extract higher-resolution capability from a *frozen* base model at inference time, in a coarse-to-fine manner (generate accurate semantics early at low resolution, then "truncate and relay" to higher resolution for texture detail). Every one of these methods encodes the irreversible coarse→fine transition as a **single, global, content-agnostic, open-loop scalar** — one relay timestep, one dilation onset, one structural switch — fired uniformly in space and without ever checking whether the early semantics are correct. Yet the failure these methods fight (semantic deviation and object repetition from "irreparable" early-semantic errors, as observed in eDiff-I's *semantics-early, details-late* behavior) is **heterogeneous across space and across prompts** and is committed *without verification*. We name this shared blind spot **Semantic-Readiness Heterogeneity (SRH)** and propose **SENTRY**: a tuning-free controller that replaces the global open-loop relay with a per-region, semantic-readiness-**triggered**, closed-loop-**verified** relay, where both the readiness signal and the repetition (duplication) signal are read *for free* from cross-attention the U-Net already computes. SENTRY adds zero trainable parameters, composes orthogonally with MegaFusion's dilated convolutions and noise re-scheduling, and **provably collapses to MegaFusion** when its signals are uninformative.

**Here are the five core questions:**

## [Question 1] - What is the problem?

Can replacing the single global, content-agnostic, open-loop coarse→fine relay used by tuning-free higher-resolution diffusion methods with a spatially-adaptive, semantic-readiness-triggered, closed-loop-verified per-region relay — where per-region readiness and object-duplication signals are estimated for free from the frozen model's cross-attention — measurably reduce semantic deviation and object repetition (FID_b, CLIP-T, CIDEr, and an open-vocabulary object-repetition rate) on hard upscaling settings while remaining measurably non-inferior to MegaFusion when those signals are uninformative, all with no retraining?

## [Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?

Tuning-free higher-resolution synthesis is one of the few levers that *democratizes* megapixel generation: retraining a diffusion model natively at 2K/4K is prohibitive in data, compute, and carbon, so the entire line of work — MegaFusion [arXiv:2408.11001], ScaleCrafter [arXiv:2310.07702], DemoFusion [arXiv:2311.16973], FouriScale [arXiv:2403.12963], HiDiffusion [arXiv:2311.17528], AccDiffusion [arXiv:2407.10738] — exists to extract high-resolution capability from a *frozen* base model at inference time. This is economically central (4K/8K assets, print, design, on-device generation on commodity GPUs) precisely because the alternative — high-resolution retraining — is out of reach for almost everyone. SENTRY inherits this constraint as non-negotiable: it adds *zero* trainable parameters and reads only signals the frozen model already computes. Its importance therefore is not "a better high-res trick" but a reframing of *what the whole subfield has been optimizing*. Every method above encodes the irreversible coarse→fine transition as a **single, global, content-agnostic, open-loop scalar** — one timestep (MegaFusion's relay), one dilation onset (ScaleCrafter/FouriScale), one structural switch (HiDiffusion), one fixed progressive schedule (DemoFusion). Naming **Semantic-Readiness Heterogeneity (SRH)** turns that shared scalar into a recognizable, named confound: the failure these methods fight (semantic deviation, object repetition from "irreparable" early-semantic errors) is *heterogeneous across space and across prompts*, yet the control variable they tune is uniform and fired blind. The contribution is a **lens**, not a hack — once SRH is named, a pile of method-specific fixes (kernel dilation, structural switching, per-patch prompts) becomes legible as crude approximations to one missing capability: a *measured, spatial, closed-loop* relay.

The importance compounds because the two ingredients SENTRY needs are **free, reusable diagnostics**. The per-region readiness signal (cross-attention temporal stability × entropy convergence) answers a question no prior method posed: *when, and where, are semantics locked in?* The duplication signature (one text token attending to multiple disjoint high-mass blobs) answers: *has a region already committed the repetition error we are trying to avoid?* Both are extracted from cross-attention the U-Net already produces, so they are zero-marginal-cost probes that any coarse→fine, cascaded, or patch-fusion method can adopt independently of SENTRY's relay machinery. This matters for future research direction: the field has a long history of using cross-attention for *editing* and *fidelity at base resolution* (Prompt-to-Prompt [arXiv:2208.01626], Attend-and-Excite [arXiv:2301.13826]), but never as a **per-region readiness clock for relay timing**. SENTRY repurposes a well-understood internal structure for a new control problem, which is the kind of move that tends to generalize.

Beyond the immediate metrics, SENTRY introduces a *transferable pattern*: **verify-then-commit at an irreversible boundary**. Any coarse→fine pipeline that makes a one-way decision — video super-resolution cascades, 3D/NeRF coarse-to-fine refinement, cascaded text-to-image (DeepFloyd IF, Imagen), latent upscaling chains — currently commits the low-resolution semantic layout *open-loop* and inherits whatever errors it baked in. Closed-loop verification before the commit is a general safety/quality pattern that is orthogonal to the specific generator. SENTRY is the concrete demonstration that this pattern is *cheap* (the verifier is the model's own attention plus an optional CLIP/VLM crop check) and *do-no-harm-bounded* (it provably collapses to the open-loop baseline when signals are uninformative). That combination — universal applicability, zero retraining, and a measured non-inferiority floor — is what makes it interesting to the broader community rather than to high-res specialists alone.

Finally, the knowledge advanced is a concrete bridge between an *observation* and a *control law*. eDiff-I [arXiv:2211.01324] established the now-canonical "semantics early, details late" behavior of the reverse diffusion trajectory, but treated it as a temporal property of the *whole* image (motivating expert denoisers across noise levels). SENTRY makes the same observation **spatial and measurable**: semantics lock in *at different timesteps in different regions*, and that field can be read off cross-attention and acted on. The practical payoff lands exactly on the regime MegaFusion still fails — large upscale factors and multi-object/compositional/counting prompts, where its own reported degradation (128→512: FID_b 88.56, CLIP-T 0.2821) is the SRH failure made quantitative. Fragile, repetition-prone regions get a *later, verified* relay; easy regions relay early and keep their detail budget. The claim that this *reduces* FID_b and *improves* CLIP-T / CIDEr on hard settings is **[conjectured→measured]**, not asserted; the value of the framing does not depend on a guaranteed win, but on giving the field a measurable axis it did not previously have.

## [Question 3] - Why is it hard?

The naive reading is "just relay each region when it looks ready." That is wrong on seven concrete, independent counts. Each is a place a naive approach silently fails.

**(1) Irreparability: proactive control on a noisy signal, no post-hoc fix.** The truncate-and-relay step is a one-way commit: once a region is upsampled and high-res denoising begins, the early coarse semantics — wrong object count, duplicated instance — are baked into the high-frequency structure and are not recoverable by any later step in the frozen sampler. There is no clean post-hoc repair (inpainting/re-rolling breaks tuning-free composability). So the controller cannot be reactive; it must predict, mid-denoising, *before* the commit, whether a region's semantics are correct, from a cross-attention signal that is intrinsically noisy, partially formed, and non-stationary. This is proactive control under uncertainty, not measure-then-cleanup.

**(2) Cross-attention is a noisy, entangled clock.** Attention maps are low-resolution (16×16, 32×32) → per-pixel relay is numerically unstable; multi-head/multi-layer disagreement means a raw average is dominated by irrelevant heads; the layout itself drifts (a region can re-snap to a different token late), so "stable for one step" ≠ "ready" — we need stability sustained over a window `w`, trading latency against false readiness; and token→region is many-to-many, so entropy convergence (assignment sharpening) is needed because raw stability can be high while the semantic assignment is still ambiguous. A signal that is simultaneously stable AND converged AND low-variance is the core estimator-design difficulty.

**(3) Leakage: the signal could collapse to the global timestep.** Attention sharpens roughly monotonically with `t`, so a poorly-built `R_t(ρ)` could just track the global timestep, firing at nearly the same `t` everywhere. Then `τ(ρ)` is a constant `≈ τ*` in disguise — we recover MegaFusion while *claiming* spatial adaptivity. The hardness is proving the signal carries region-specific information beyond the global clock; the Leakage ablation regresses `τ(ρ)` on global `t` and demands a non-trivial region-specific residual that correlates with per-region failure. Without it every downstream win is unattributable.

**(4) Region definition without retraining, and regions interact.** No free ground-truth segmentation exists at inference. Fixed grid cuts objects and puts seams mid-object; cross-attention token-blob clusters are semantically aligned but unstable early (chicken-and-egg with the thing we measure); superpixels add cost and over-segment texture. Worse, regions are not independent: if region A defers (stays low-res) while neighbor B relays to high-res, we hold a multi-resolution latent/feature field and must stitch it coherently — naive stitching gives seams, boundary-frequency artifacts, resolution-mismatch halos. Coherent tuning-free multi-resolution stitching is itself non-trivial.

**(5) Latent-space entanglement: "per-region" isn't cleanly local.** In latent diffusion (SD 2.1/SDXL) the VAE couples spatial locations and convolution mixes neighbors every step, so a per-region relay in latent space leaks across boundaries — it is not cleanly local. In pixel space (DeepFloyd IF) locality is much cleaner, so the same mechanism is easier there and harder in latent space. The method must degrade gracefully under entanglement (dilate masks, feather boundaries, fall back via the do-no-harm gate) rather than assume clean locality — which is why we evaluate both backbones.

**(6) Tuning-free verification cost/calibration; re-weighting can destabilize the sampler.** A per-step VLM/CLIP check is expensive and its thresholds (`θ_dup`, `θ_clip`) must work across prompts with no per-prompt tuning — mis-set, they either never fire or defer everything (hit `t_floor`, wasting compute, over-smoothing). So we lean on a free attention duplication signature and use the VLM check sparingly. And corrective re-weighting is an intervention the frozen model never trained to receive: over-suppression can delete a legitimate object (false-positive duplicate), shift global energy, or push the latent off-manifold. The intervention must be conservative, renormalizing, reversible-until-commit, and ablatable.

**(7) The failure is heterogeneous and standard metrics can't see it.** Object repetition and per-region deviation are local, instance-level failures; FID/CLIP-T are global statistics that weakly correlate with "the lion appears three times." A method could move FID without touching repetition, or vice-versa — so FID/CLIP-T alone cannot attribute or even detect the targeted effect. The difficulty extends to measurement design: we must add a dedicated region/instance-level repetition metric (open-vocabulary detector counting instances vs. prompt-specified count). Reusing global metrics on a local failure is a methodological trap.

**Bottom line.** The difficulty is the conjunction of: an irreversible commit forcing proactive control; on a noisy, entangled signal; that must be proven region-specific (anti-leakage); over interacting regions needing coherent multi-resolution stitching; under latent entanglement; with tuning-free, sampler-safe verification/intervention; and a new metric because the target failure is invisible to standard global metrics. None is incidental — each is exactly why "just relay when ready" fails, and each maps to a specific design choice and ablation rung.

## [Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?

The honest answer is that the subfield never *modeled* the heterogeneity, because it framed the coarse→fine transition as a single hyperparameter. Walk the prior art and the same structural limitation appears in each, just dressed differently. **MegaFusion** [arXiv:2408.11001] uses one global truncate-and-relay timestep `τ*`: identical for fragile and easy regions, fired without ever checking whether the early semantics are correct before the irreversible high-resolution commit — global, content-agnostic, *open-loop*. **ScaleCrafter** [arXiv:2310.07702] enlarges the convolutional receptive field via kernel re-dilation to suppress object repetition, but the dilation is **uniform** and its onset is global and fixed; there is no per-region relay timing and no closed-loop semantic check. **DemoFusion** [arXiv:2311.16973] runs a global, *fixed* progressive-upscaling schedule with skip-residual and dilated sampling — many passes (slow), global guidance only, no per-region readiness, no verification. **FouriScale** [arXiv:2403.12963] imposes a global frequency-domain dilation plus low-pass for structure/scale consistency: spatially uniform and open-loop. **HiDiffusion** [arXiv:2311.17528] correctly traces duplication to deep-U-Net feature duplication and flips a global **structural switch** (resolution-aware U-Net + window attention) at a fixed depth — architectural and content-agnostic, with no readiness signal and no verification. **AccDiffusion** [arXiv:2407.10738] is the closest in spirit: it makes the *prompt* patch-content-aware to fight repetition — but it edits the **conditioning**, not the **relay schedule**; its denoising timing stays global and fixed, and it never verifies residual duplication *before* committing the high-res step. So every method either keeps timing global, keeps the loop open, or both.

Four concrete barriers kept the gap open. **(a) The transition was a scalar.** The field's mental model was "pick the right relay timestep / dilation onset / switch depth," a one-dimensional hyperparameter search; under that model, *spatial* heterogeneity is literally unrepresentable, so it was never even an object of study. **(b) Cross-attention was claimed by a different problem.** Prompt-to-Prompt [arXiv:2208.01626] and Attend-and-Excite [arXiv:2301.13826] established cross-attention as a handle for *editing* and for *fidelity at base resolution* (re-weighting to make all subject tokens appear). That framing was so successful that cross-attention came to mean "manipulate the output," not "read a per-region **readiness clock** for when to relay." Repurposing the same maps as a timing signal at the coarse→fine boundary is a different question that the editing literature had no reason to ask. **(c) "Errors are irreparable" was treated as a premise, not a target.** The whole motivation for coarse→fine — incorrect early semantics lock in irreparable high-res errors — was accepted as a fixed fact, so the design response was "schedule carefully," never "*pre-empt the error by verifying before committing*." Closed-loop verification was implicitly assumed too costly for an inference-time, tuning-free method. **(d) There was no region-level repetition metric.** You cannot model a heterogeneous failure you cannot localize; existing metrics (FID, CLIP-T, CIDEr) are image-global, so the spatial structure of the failure was invisible and therefore un-optimizable.

SENTRY differs by attacking exactly those barriers, and the distinction should be stated cleanly. **First**, it makes relay **timing a measured spatial field `τ(ρ)`** instead of a scalar `τ*` — derived for free from cross-attention temporal stability and entropy convergence — so heterogeneity becomes representable and actionable; no prior method makes timing per-region or driven by a *measured* readiness signal. **Second**, it **closes the loop**: a duplication signature (one token → multiple disjoint blobs) plus an optional cheap CLIP/VLM crop check verifies semantics are *correct*, not merely *stable*, and on failure *defers* the relay or applies corrective attention re-weighting — verify-then-commit at the irreversible boundary, which nobody above does. Both knobs are **orthogonal** (each ablates independently) and both are **tuning-free**, reading only frozen-model signals.

The epistemic ledger stays disciplined so Q4 does not over-claim. The boundary-case reductions — SENTRY collapsing exactly to MegaFusion (both knobs off), to direct-upsample, to base-resolution, and to the single-region / verification-off / spatial-off isolations — are **[provable-by-construction]**: they follow from the definitions, no measurement needed, and they double as the do-no-harm property that SENTRY can only *add* capability, never silently degrade the baseline by construction at the degenerate limit. The readiness estimator `R_t(ρ)` and the duplication estimator `D(ρ)` are **[designed]** instruments whose fidelity to real per-region semantic lock-in and real repetition is **[conjectured→measured]**, not assumed. The headline effects — reduced FID_b and improved CLIP-T / CIDEr on hard upscaling settings, and non-inferiority within ε vs MegaFusion when readiness signals are uninformative — are **[conjectured→measured]**; the non-inferiority is explicitly a *measured* property under the do-no-harm gate, **never a guarantee**. In short: the gap stayed open because the field optimized *where the kernel looks* and never asked *when each region is ready* nor *checked before committing*; SENTRY is the first to make relay timing a measured spatial field **and** to close the loop, both for free and with no retraining.

## [Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?

**Pipeline (end to end, frozen model, inference-only).** (1) Low-res denoise at base resolution `r₀` as in MegaFusion, but instrument cross-attention; (2) form regions tuning-free — cross-attention token-blob clusters with a fixed-grid fallback, dilated/feathered to absorb latent entanglement; (3) per-step, per-region readiness from cross-attention — stability `σ_t(ρ) = 1 − (Σ_k ‖Ā_t^{(k)}(ρ) − Ā_{t+Δ}^{(k)}(ρ)‖₁)/Z` (layout stops moving) × entropy convergence `H_t(ρ)` flattening (`|ΔH| ≤ η`), giving `R_t(ρ) = σ_t(ρ)·1[|ΔH_t(ρ)| ≤ η]`, ready when `≥ θ_ready` sustained over window `w`; (4) closed-loop verification before commit — free duplication signature `D(ρ) = max_k (|disjoint high-mass blobs of token k overlapping ρ| − n*_k)₊` plus optional cheap CLIP/VLM presence `c(ρ)`, with `V = [D ≤ θ_dup] ∧ [c ≥ θ_clip]`; (5) readiness+verification-**triggered** per-region relay `τ(ρ) = max{t : R ready over w AND V = PASS}` else floor `t_floor` — fragile regions relay late, easy regions early (freeing detail budget); on `V = FAIL` defer and/or conservatively re-weight (down-weight duplicate blobs, renormalize) *before* the irreversible step; (6) coherent multi-resolution stitching (feathered, frequency-matched); (7) continue high-res denoising with MegaFusion's dilated convolutions + noise re-scheduling active (SENTRY **composes with**, does not replace, them); (8) do-no-harm gate `g = 1[Var_ρ(τ(ρ)) ≤ ν OR contrast(A_t) ≤ ν]` → collapse `τ(ρ) → τ*`, disable re-weighting = exactly MegaFusion **[provable-by-construction]**; non-inferiority within ε is **measured, never guaranteed**.

**Backbones (MegaFusion coverage, apples-to-apples).** Stable Diffusion 2.1 (latent), SDXL (latent), DeepFloyd IF (pixel-space, clean-locality contrast), and a ControlNet conditional extension.

**Datasets.** MS-COCO (FID + CIDEr/Meteor/ROUGE via captioning the generated image and comparing to the prompt); T2I-CompBench (compositional/multi-object/counting); and a curated "repetition-prone" set (multiple distinct objects, counting prompts, large aspect ratios). Resolutions/upscale factors matching MegaFusion: 512→1024, 512→2048, 64→256, 64→512, and the headline hard regime 128→512 (where MegaFusion reports FID_b 88.56, CLIP-T 0.2821).

**Baselines (same-backbone, compute-matched).** Base model (direct high-res); direct upsampling/SR; MegaFusion (primary); ScaleCrafter; DemoFusion; FouriScale; HiDiffusion; AccDiffusion.

**Metrics — MegaFusion suite (parity).** FID_r (vs real), FID_b (vs base-resolution output = base-consistency), KID_r, KID_b, CLIP-T, CIDEr/Meteor/ROUGE, GFlops, inference time. **New targeted metrics.** (i) **Object-repetition rate** via an open-vocabulary detector (e.g., GroundingDINO) — detected instances vs prompt-specified count (count error, spurious-duplicate rate) — the headline SRH metric; (ii) **semantic-presence/deviation** via VQA or CLIP object-presence; (iii) **compute-matched quality** — FID_b/CLIP-T/repetition at equal GFlops and equal wall-clock (a compute–quality Pareto); (iv) **human evaluation** (semantic accuracy + detail quality), as MegaFusion did.

**Ablation ladder.** *Boundary-case sanity checks* **[provable-by-construction]** (must reproduce bit-exactly): MegaFusion (`τ = τ*`, verify-off), direct-upsample (`τ = T`), base-res (`τ = 0`), single-region adaptive-global, verification-off (Knob 1 only), spatial-off (Knob 2 only). *2×2 knob factorial:* spatial ∈ {off, on} × verify ∈ {off, on}; the (off, off) corner = MegaFusion, (on, on) = full SENTRY — isolates each knob's marginal contribution and orthogonality. *Four rungs:* **Oracle** (relay each region exactly when an offline decodability probe says its final class is fixed — upper bound on timing); **Placebo** (random per-region relay times, matched marginal — controls for "any heterogeneity helps"; SENTRY must beat it); **Recovery** (on the subset of prompts MegaFusion fails, does closed-loop verification recover them — isolates Knob 2); **Leakage** (regress `τ(ρ)`/`R_t(ρ)` on global `t`; report region-specific residual and its correlation with per-region failure — direct test against Q3.3).

**Expected outcomes [conjectured→measured] (targets, not guarantees).** On the hard regime (large upscale, multi-object — e.g., 128→512): lower object-repetition rate + lower FID_b + higher CLIP-T/CIDEr than MegaFusion at equal or lower GFlops; do-no-harm — within ε of MegaFusion on easy single-object prompts (gate fires); a compute–quality Pareto improvement (easy regions relay early freeing budget, fragile regions relay late only where needed); and attribution — SENTRY > Placebo (the signal matters, not mere heterogeneity), a non-trivial Leakage residual, Recovery showing Knob 2 rescues MegaFusion's failures, and a factorial showing both knobs contribute. Reductions-to-prior-methods are **[provable-by-construction]**; improvements and non-inferiority are **[conjectured→measured]**.

**Ethics & responsible use.** Higher-resolution generative capability lowers the cost of photoreal synthetic imagery; we will (a) restrict releases to research artifacts and attach provenance/watermark metadata (e.g., C2PA), (b) report demographic-slice quality so that "fragile region" handling does not systematically degrade people-centric content, (c) keep the optional VLM verifier auditable and avoid using it to silently filter benign content, (d) note that SENTRY does not certify factual correctness — it reduces *measured* repetition/deviation, not real-world truth, and (e) acknowledge that SENTRY runs on SD/SDXL/LAION-derived models and inherits their documented biases (stereotyping, demographic underrepresentation, training-data consent limitations); higher resolution only amplifies the visual salience of these biases without correcting them. We explicitly oppose using SENTRY to generate non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII) or synthetic media intended for political disinformation; C2PA provenance tagging is a deterrent, not a technical bar.

**Q1↔Q5 epistemic consistency.** Q5 delivers exactly what Q1 claims — provable boundary constructions + measured effects + measured non-inferiority + no retraining — neither under-delivering nor over-claiming any guarantee.
